name: Stir Fry Chicken Szechuan Style
recipeIngredient: 1 pound Bok choy or Chinese cabbage, cut into bite-size pieces
1 tablespoon Bean paste (or hot bean paste)
2 teaspoons Oil (such as sesame or vegetable oil)
1 teaspoon Cornstarch dissolved in 1 tablespoon cold water
recipeInstructions:
1. Marinate the chicken for 20 minutes using the marinade ingredients.
2. Heat a wok over high heat until hot. Add 2 tablespoons of oil, then sprinkle salt.
3. Add Bok choy and stir-fry for 1 minute.
4. Add 1/2 cup of chicken stock. Cover and cook for 3 minutes. Remove to a serving plate and set aside.
5. Heat the wok again. Add 2 tablespoons of oil, then add ginger, garlic, and bean paste. Cook for 1-2 minutes until the chicken is slightly browned.
6. Add in all the seasonings. Pour in the remaining stock. Cover and cook for 1 minute.
7. Stir in the cornstarch mixture to thicken the sauce.
8. Remove from heat and place on top of the Bok choy. Sprinkle scallions on top and serve.
